title: Data and Records Management Procedure
preamble: This procedure defines requirements for managing HSESC data and records — from creation through to secure retention, accessibility, and disposal — ensuring that the information generated by the project management system is reliable, defensible, and available for the purposes of assurance, regulatory compliance, and learning.
owns: Data ownership and authorised storage requirements
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0010 – Document Control (document types subject to retention requirements)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0010 references RTPR-HSE-PRO-0043 for record retention
All procedures producing records reference RTPR-HSE-PRO-0043 for retention periods

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – General Requirements Standard]
requirements.text: Require that all environmental and HSESC data is validated, accurate, current, and defensible, with use of accredited laboratories where required. Require annual data analysis and interpretation at minimum. Document data assumptions, limitations, and uncertainty.
Implement a data quality assurance programme with defined QA/QC protocols, sampling standards, and validation steps proportionate to the sensitivity of the data and its intended uses.

requirements.tag: [TSM – TSM – Tailings Management Protocol, Indicator TM3]
requirements.text: Ensure that data used for tailings and geotechnical monitoring is collected, validated, and stored in a manner that supports independent verification and long-term accessibility.

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – General Requirements Standard]
requirements.text: Require that all environmental and HSESC data is owned by RT, stored on authorised company systems in a format ensuring long-term integrity and accessibility, and not held solely on contractor or service provider systems.
Require that community, stakeholder, and personal data is managed in compliance with the RT Data Privacy Standard, with appropriate access controls and confidentiality protections.
Retain monitoring data and management records for the life of the project and beyond where legacy environmental obligations exist.

Define minimum retention periods by record category: electrical equipment inspection records — minimum 5 years; incident investigation records — per classification and applicable legislation; explosives records — minimum 5 years or life of mine; environmental monitoring datasets — life of asset; training and competency records — employment period plus a defined post-employment period. Define the authority for approving early disposal of records.

The project must define a records disposal process for records that have reached their retention expiry, documenting: what was destroyed; the authority that approved disposal; the disposal date and method; and any records that were transferred to a permanent archive rather than disposed of.

Disclose environmental and social monitoring reports to affected communities in a manner and format that is accessible and understandable, consistent with applicable performance standard disclosure obligations.

The project must define access rights by role for all HSESC data and records, classifying information by sensitivity (public; project-restricted; commercially confidential; personal/medical), and document access control procedures for each classification.

project_completion_guidance: Define the authorised data storage platforms for this project: which system holds HSESC records; which holds environmental monitoring data; which holds community engagement records. The procedure requires authorised systems — the project defines the systems.
Define the project records classification system: which record types are public; project-restricted; commercially confidential; personal/medical. Define access controls for each.
Confirm any project-specific retention requirements that exceed the minimum periods in this procedure (e.g., longer retention in the project host jurisdiction). Insert as a table.
Define the project records disposal authority: who can approve disposal of records at the end of their retention period; what documentation of disposal is required.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – General Requirements Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires all environmental and HSESC data to be validated, owned by RT, stored on authorised systems, with annual analysis and documented assumptions.
Requires community, stakeholder, and personal data to be managed in compliance with the RT Data Privacy Standard with appropriate access controls.

Defines minimum record retention periods by category including electrical inspection records, incident investigation records, explosives records, environmental monitoring datasets, and training records.
Requires a data quality assurance programme with QA/QC protocols, retention of monitoring data and management records for the life of the project, and proportionate validation steps.
Requires disclosure of environmental and social monitoring reports to affected communities in an accessible and understandable manner consistent with applicable performance standard obligations.
Requires that data used for tailings and geotechnical monitoring is collected, validated, and stored to support independent verification and long-term accessibility.
Requires data records to be maintained, reviewed, and interpreted to a level of rigour that defends business reputation and meets external reporting commitments.
Requires environmental information, communications, and system documents to be accessible by the project workforce in a format and location accessible to implementing personnel.
Requires all geospatial and monitoring data to be provided in the correct local grid reference and in a format importable into Company spatial data management systems.
